ogTitle: Babbage Island vs Badgebup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Babbage Island and Badgebup.
heading: Babbage Island vs Badgebup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Babbage Island, Western Australia and Badgebup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Babbage Island has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Badgebup (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Babbage Island or Badgebup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Babbage Island has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Badgebup scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Babbage Island scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Badgebup scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Babbage Island or Badgebup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Babbage Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
